reviews.review_description: It's a challenging linear platformer similar to crash bandicoot.  Good graphics and audio.  Solid mechanics and overall just a great platformer.  VR needs more 3d platformers like this.  Highly recommend!

If you are expecting the innovative mechanics of Max Mustard, or the engaging story and interesting puzzles of Moss, then you will be disappointed, but if you just want a cool platformer in VR with a Crash Bandicoot vibe, then this game delivers that. The graphics of the first levels are pretty good, though at later levels you can see some textures that look a bit more basic (but overall I'd say the graphics of the game are good). I didn't mind the fixed camera angles like some other people complained, but the game also has a different camera option where the camera follows the character if people prefer that. The game starts fairly easy, but as you progress the game becomes significantly harder (especially in the last world levels where you need to time your jumps at very small platforms that are super easy to miss), so that may be a positive or a negative depending if you like a very challenging game. The game has nothing that stands out in VR mechanics like Moss games or Max Mustard, and even though the game has some sections that tries to make use of the motion controllers, these sections are few and far between and they are underwhelming in my opinion. The platformer itself is good though, and as the environments look good it's a solid platformer to play in VR. If you like platformers in VR and already played the other more popular titles (e.g. Moss 1 & 2, Max Mustard, Lucky's Tale) then I think it's a good game to get.
Great graphics, very fun game, reminds me about Crash Bandicoot but in Virtual Reality.
Good game, epic graphics - it could be an amazing game but the character moves too slowly in comparison to the obstacles which is super frustrating

reviews.review_title: Fun at first, annoying further on
reviews.review_description:
Starts out pretty good, with decent graphics and a gradual learning curve. But the further in, the more the wonky collisions and imprecise controls get in the way of the fun. The timing on a lot of moving obstacles is too quick for how slowly Ven moves. And one hit kills in a platformer just ain't it.

Levels have some well hidden collectibles and there are a few neat set pieces, so it's not all bad.

Overall though, it's promising, but too unpolished.

Just straight up the best platform there is for quest nothing comes to those people have talked about the camera and goals of being weird which I kind of agree but that is more in my experience due to it just being 3-D issue with how far distances you are/objects blocking view.

I really enjoyed this game.  Level design was fun and snappy.  I hold the same critiques as others regarding camera angles, like looking down or sideways too much.  I played it on easy mode which gives plenty of chances to clear the stage.

Absolutely love how this game is made — the characters are super cool, the level design is creative, and everything is beautifully textured and animated. Collecting the assets is genuinely fun, and the game pulls you in from the very beginning with its immersive feel.

One big improvement would be the camera system. If it followed the character more closely, like in Lucky’s Tale, it would be a lot easier to stay connected to Ven. Right now, the camera often pulls too far back or shifts angles unpredictably, which makes it hard to see where the character is and judge jumps accurately. It’s easy to feel detached because of the perspective changes, and I find myself failing sometimes simply due to those awkward camera angles.

That said, the difficulty so far feels balanced. The first few levels are very manageable and enjoyable to play through.

reviews.review_title: A decent game that quickly frustrates
reviews.review_description:
A VR third-person platforming game in which you control Ven the protagonist as if you are a higher power. The game relies on a fixed third person perspective that doesn't pan with the player unless you advance in the level. The camera is rotatable, but it doesn't rotate with the character at a fixed point. You guide Ven through various levels attacking enemies that lack variation. Going from A to B in super long levels isn't enough, as you are also tasked to save all the mini dog things, break all of the boxes along the way and to collect all of the relics, which are essentially little gems scattered around the course and in boxes. Some of them are very well hidden. 

I think the game itself shows promise on a genre that isn't typically designed for VR and the third person view is an adequate solution. However, nothing about this game is truly exemplary. The protagonist has voice lines that really only activate when you die and then resurrect. The difficulty of the platforming itself isn't hard at all, with a circle referencing where your character will land and it is extremely easy to accumulate extra lives. The game suddenly spikes in difficulty, not in skill but in sheer diminishing controls.

The truth about this game is that if it were released on PC or Console it would be embarrassing to play. Nothing about the game truly uses VR effectively with the exception of the level select screen, which works well but seems to be composed of premade assets. It's unimaginative and uninspiring. And although I am nearly done with the game, nothing makes me excited to go back to it and test my platforming prowess. Especially with a part of the game that is insanely hard and unnecessary: lobbing grenades at rockets that propel when above you. It's awful, and turned me off from finishing the game.

I would exercise caution if the game wasn't on a steep discount and would look for alternatives. See if Moss, Lucky's Tale, Max Mustard or even No More Rainbows tickles your fancy.

An underrated gem I rarely see mentioned.  If you like more traditional 3rd person platforming, then give Ven a try. It's in the same vein as Moss or Max Mustard though a bit harder than both of those.  Visually the main course looks very nice and has lots of diverse environments and obstacles, but could use an update to increase resolution for the newer Quests.  Gameplay is great too, and I appreciate how they changed up the platforming mechanics and difficulty the further you go.  Expect to dedicate multiple attempts for some of the harder sections, but thankfully there are generous amounts of checkpoints.  In contrast to that generosity, it also possess a limited life system and while I never felt pressured by it on Normal difficulty, I could see some people feeling frustrated if they struggle with platformers and need to reset a lot.
I can’t get in my game it won’t start

Very good game, graphics great, inventive obstacles, fun. 
The But. Very very difficult, and the restart of levels. Fix these two areas the game would be great. Have options to make easier, I can’t get halfway through, would love to be able to finish game. Just to frustrating.

This is by far the BEST platformer available on the Quest Store…

Yes, it’s BETTER than Max Mustard… For a variety of reasons.

1) This game is challenging (To me, this is a GOOD thing. I always love a good challenge)

2) The environments and levels are interesting and quite varied. For example, snow areas, factory areas, cave areas etc…

3) Gameplay itself is great, feels AAA. Everything feels clean and smooth and works well.

4) Great Voice acting.

The only negative in my opinion is that the backgrounds of the levels are sort of 2d wallpaperish and not full 3D HD renders. This breaks immersion a bit.

My daughter and I were both fans of Max Mustard and thought this game looked to be similar. In many ways it is - although the camera angles are at times not quite so good - but for the first few levels we thoroughly enjoyed the game. We're not expert gamers and until we got towards the end of the ice castle level, it seemed to be pitched at the right level for us.  However, there's a section on the ice castle that neither of us can get past. It's the bit where you have to keep up with a moving platform, but there are walls in your way to go around. Try as we might, we can't seem to jump round these.  When you've exhausted all your lives trying and need to restart the level from the beginning, it's frustrating, and I can see Ven being uninstalled to make room for something else.

The save points throughout the levels are good but only work while you are within the level. It is a shame that when you exit, you can't restart the level at the save point but have to restart it from scratch again.

That's what it reminds me of is crash bandicoot when it first came out on PlayStation 2 with 3D graphics I wish Ben had a few more fighting moves about him but other than that it's fun and I recommend changing the camera view to move freely
…on the Quest 2, so colourful, amazing graphics for a platform game and cool music and effects and some very challenging levels, love little Ven, such a cutie, buy it you won’t regret it..
I really like the game but I think it would be way better if you were actually the little guy not just floating out in space!

I am an average player and I find this game really enjoyable.  I wish there were more like it. 

The bosses were not super hard to beat but still challenging enough to cause some frustration ha ha!

I can see myself playing this game several times and would recommend it for anyone.

This game can be infuriating!  You have to PERFECTLY perform several very challenging steps just to get to another super challenging set of obstacles you need to get past in order to get a save option on the easiest setting. As many other people have said, the camera angles are somtimes horrible where ven completely diasappears and you dont know if there is an enemy or a jump or what to do. The fix they provide is much worse.  This game is what I was afraid it would be.  I'm not a gamer and didn't know what a platform game was. I'm of course not holding this against them. It just takes me back to my chilhood of playing Mario and getting to the same spot and never getting passed it.  Im stuck at the end of level 4 i think it is where you are in a moving platform and have to get around a set of walls.  I have no idea how to do that, even after watching videos. I had to die probably 20 times just to get to that point, then i die and have to do it all over again. After a while i quit and have to replay the whole level again.

The good. The controls are easy.  I really like the graphics and I'd really like to see the rest of the game, but havent been able to bring myself to attempt to play in weeks because it's so frustrating.

If Ven is on Sale do yourself a favor and buy it. At full price though there are better options.

Some like platform games in VR others don’t see the purpose. I personally love a good VR platformer.  While Moss is the standard Lucky’s Tale is the polished big studio gem, Ven is a beautiful platformer from a small studio that lacks something o can’t put my finger on. 

They fixed the camera controls that were a main complaint upon release. The worlds are beautiful and varied and the gameplay is just perfect. 

Ven could use more “actions” he jumps, he double jumps, and he can punch. That’s it. This game could use varied power up and actions like Banjo Kazoo or even Mario. While the enemies are varied they are all easily beat with the same joystick input. 

Ven is easy almost too easy. I like when a good platformer has multiple paths and isn’t super linear, or has hidden areas with power up, and vr lends to being very creative with diversity in play through. I wish there was a vr platformer that had puzzles built into the level .  Ven is very much the same thing jump, jump, spin attack, jump.

I’m waiting for a platformer to take what Moss started and add to it. Making the player the center of the universe not the character. 

What Ven does well. Ven does very well. The levels despite being very linear are well designed and gorgeous. The controls work well and there are about 3-4 hours of gameplay if you go slow. If you play just to get to the end you will have Ven beat in about an hour. 

Because Ven lacks in the areas listed above there is little replay value. In my first play through I was 1 or 2 items short of collecting every collectible and I am not usually one that finds everything on a first play through. I like to play through at a slower pace but not working hard on collectibles. This is so I can go back and really explore, finding hidden areas and 100% a level. This adds value to me as I get more in game time. Ven on the other hand was nearly 100% Ed with ease. 

Ven is a solid 4 stars for many reasons but far from perfect for others. My advice is if you liked Moss, Lucky’s tale is a must have and Ven takes third place for platform games on Quest.
